ogTitle: Sweers Island vs Taabinga: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sweers Island and Taabinga.
heading: Sweers Island vs Taabinga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sweers Island, Queensland and Taabinga, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sweers Island has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Taabinga (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sweers Island or Taabinga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sweers Island has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Taabinga scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sweers Island scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Taabinga scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sweers Island or Taabinga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sweers Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
